ocr.text: The data presented in the table elucidates the effects of varying concentrations of organic acids and sulfate on the production of
key metabolites, specifically focusing on the metrics for acetate and sulfide. In the context of a controlled experiment involving
wild-type (WT) and mutant (Mut) variants, the introduction of 20 mM lactate and 21 mM sulfate serves as a critical variable
influencing metabolic outcomes. Notably, the mean acetate concentration observed in the WT variant was 10.3 mM, while the
Mut variant exhibited a slightly lower mean of 10.0 mM, indicating potential differences in metabolic efficiency or pathway
utilization between the two genotypes. Furthermore, the sulfide levels, measured in millimolar concentrations, provide insight
into the sulfur metabolism under these experimental conditions.
Strain, genotype  Initial culture medium  Remaining substrate (mM)  Product formed
Organic acid  Sulfatea  Sulfide (mM)a  Acetate (mM)a  Formate (mM)  H2 (μmole)
WT  20 mM lactate, 21 mM sulfate  NDb  10.3  10.0  17.1  0.1  0.1
JW9087, ΔcycA  20 mM lactate, 21 mM sulfate  NDb  12.0  8.3  18.5  1.6  79.5
VKORC1 SNP  HGSV  Haplotype
WT variant  Mut variant
Measurement  Left  Right  p values
N (number of hands)  Mean  Median  SD  N (number of hands)  Mean  Median  SD
The inclusion of metrics such as the standard deviation (SD) and median values for each variant enhances the robustness of the
data, allowing for a more nuanced interpretation of the metabolic dynamics at play. This comprehensive analysis is essential for
understanding the metabolic adaptations associated with genetic variations in these organisms.
